title: Data Integrity
summary: 9618 AS — validation, verification, parity checks, and checksums to maintain accurate data.
learningObjectives: Distinguish data integrity from data security.
Suggest integrity controls for given scenarios.

simpleExplanation.title: Data stays correct
simpleExplanation.summary: Integrity ensures data is accurate and consistent. Validation checks input reasonableness; verification confirms transfer accuracy.
steps: Validation: range, type, length, format checks on entry.
Verification: double entry, visual check after transfer.
Parity bit: even/odd bit count detects single-bit errors.
Checksum: sum/hash compared before and after transmission.

sections.content: **Data integrity** (6.2) pairs with **data security** (6.1). Do not confuse **validation** (reasonable input) with **verification** (correct copy).

items: Referential integrity in DBMS — FK matches PK
Transcription errors caught by verification, not validation alone
CRC stronger than simple checksum for error detection
Backup supports integrity recovery after corruption
